summary: Comprehensive testing completed for EdgeLog Google-only authentication with deep link approach. All 20 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend verified: Login page shows ONLY Google Sign-In button (Apple Sign-In removed as requested). Native auth token storage/retrieval working correctly with MongoDB persistence.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/health returns healthy status with timestamp
Backend: GET /api/ returns EdgeLog API v1.0.0
Backend: POST /api/auth/native/store stores token in MongoDB and returns {success: true}
Backend: POST /api/auth/native/store validates required fields (returns 422 for missing fields)
Backend: POST /api/auth/native/store supports upsert (updates existing token)
Backend: POST /api/auth/native/retrieve returns stored token successfully
Backend: POST /api/auth/native/retrieve deletes token after retrieval (one-time use)
Backend: POST /api/auth/native/retrieve returns 404 for non-existent tokens
Backend: GET /api/auth/native-callback returns HTML content
Backend: GET /api/auth/native-callback has cache-control: no-cache, no-store headers
Backend: GET /api/auth/native-callback has dynamic API URL (not hardcoded)
Backend: GET /api/auth/native-callback includes auth_request_id in HTML
Backend: GET /api/auth/native-callback has EdgeLog branding
Backend: GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json returns valid JSON array
Backend: GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json has correct package: com.ravuri.edgelog
Backend: GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json has SHA256 certificate fingerprint
Backend: GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json has delegate_permission/common.handle_all_urls relation
Backend: GET /api/auth/me returns 401 without authentication
Backend: POST /api/auth/session validates session_id requirement
Backend: POST /api/auth/logout works correctly
Frontend: Login page loads correctly
Frontend: EDGELOG logo and tagline displayed
Frontend: Google Sign-In button present and enabled
Frontend: Apple Sign-In button NOT present (removed as requested)
Frontend: No button with 'Apple' text found
Frontend: Only 1 login button on page (Google only)
Frontend: Google button redirects to auth.emergentagent.com for OAuth
seed_data_creation: None - tests use temporary auth tokens that are cleaned up
context_for_next_testing_agent: Google-only authentication with deep link approach fully tested and working. Key endpoints: POST /api/auth/native/store (stores token in MongoDB), POST /api/auth/native/retrieve (one-time retrieval), GET /api/auth/native-callback (HTML callback with dynamic API URL), GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json (Android App Links). Apple Sign-In completely removed from LoginPage.js. The native auth flow uses polling (1.5s intervals) to retrieve tokens stored by the callback page.

features_verified.google_only_login: Login page shows ONLY Google Sign-In button - Apple Sign-In removed
features_verified.native_token_storage: POST /api/auth/native/store stores tokens in MongoDB with 5-minute expiry
features_verified.native_token_retrieval: POST /api/auth/native/retrieve returns token and deletes it (one-time use)
features_verified.native_callback_html: GET /api/auth/native-callback returns HTML with dynamic API URL and cache-busting headers
features_verified.android_app_links: GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json returns correct config for com.ravuri.edgelog
features_verified.web_oauth_redirect: Google button redirects to auth.emergentagent.com for web OAuth flow
